step_by_step_reasoning: We first identify the core application cluster components: ALB, Tomcat on EC2 instances, and EFS storage. Then we add Cognito for authentication and S2S VPN for customer network connectivity. Next, we map the DevOps management pipeline: S3 hosting a static SPA, API Gateway, and Lambda. Finally, we map the account management/governance layer using AWS Organizations and Control Tower. Edges are sequentialized into flows for clarity, capturing the whiteboard's exact visual flows.

graph.name: Appway Multi-Tenant SaaS Environment
graph.notes: Ensures strong customer isolation using dedicated AWS accounts per tenant, unified under AWS Organizations and managed with Control Tower. DevOps pipeline manages infrastructure deployment serverlessly.

nodes.name: Cognito
nodes.notes: Handles authentication; supports OIDC and identity federation.

nodes.name: ALB
nodes.notes: Handles request routing and SSL offloading.

nodes.name: AppWay Cluster Nodes
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Java/Tomcat web apps running across AZs, clustered via Hazelcast.

nodes.name: S2S VPN
nodes.notes: Provides private access to on-premises customer systems.

nodes.name: Customer Network
nodes.notes: Hosts customer IDP and core systems.

nodes.name: Lambda
nodes.notes: Orchestrates account creation and cluster deployment.

edges.notes: Secures data transit to customer systems.
